title: Coldries India Refractory Ltd. Katni ... vs Municipal Corporation Katni on 16 April, 2018
body: Coldries India Refractory Ltd. Katni ... vs Municipal Corporation Katni on 16 April, 2018 THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH MP-1647-2018 (COLDRIES INDIA REFRACTORY LTD. KATNI REFRACTORY WORKS Vs MUNICIPAL CORPORATION KATNI) Jabalpur, Dated : 16-04-2018 Shri Jaideep Sirpurkar, learned counsel for the petitioner. Heard. The petitioner has filed the present petition being aggrieved by the order dated 9/03/2018 passed by 1st Additional Sessions Judge, sh Katni in miscellaneous civil appeal no. 2/2017 by which the temporary e injunction granted in favour of the plaintiff has been set aside. ad The petitioner being the plaintiff filed the suit for permanent Pr injunction against the Municipal Corporation, Katni. The plaintiff is engaged in the business of refractory works over the land bearing a patwari halka no. 44/43, khasra no. 53 ad-measuring0.886 hectares. hy Petitioner is having a dormant mine on the aforesaid land in which ad water is being collected for catering of its water needs under the water M harvesting system. It is alleged that the defendant is interfering with the possession of the petitioner over the aforesaid land and trying to of draw the water from the mine by installing pipes. The aforesaid rt conduct of the respondent gave a cause of action to the plaintiff to file ou the suit to seek the relief of permanent injunction. Plaintiff filed an application under Order 39 Rules 1 and 2 of CPC . C After receipt of the summons the defendant has appeared and h filed the written statement alongwith the reply to the application for ig temporary injunction. Vide order dated 30/11/2016 the learned trial H Court has allowed the application for grant of temporary injunction and restrained the defendant from laying down the pipe lines or to interfere with the plaintiff's possession. Being aggrieved by the order of temporary injunction dated 30/11/2016 the defendant preferred a miscellaneous appeal under the provisions of Order 43 Rule 1 (r) of the CPC . The learned Additional District Judge vide order dated 9/03/2018 has set aside the order of civil court granting the temporary injunction with the condition that after laying down the pipe lines the municipal corporation shall take the water from the mine of the plaintiff subject to the order passed by the Collector under the Madhya Pradesh Peya Jal Parirakshan Adhiniyam, 1986 (hereinafter shall be referred as 'the Adhiniyam, 1986' in short). Being aggrieved by the order passed by the Additional District Judge, now the plaintiff has preferred the present petition before this Court. Undisputedly, the Collector Katni has granted the permission vide order dated 1/02/2017 under the Adhiniyam, 1986 from the period 30th June, 2017 till arrival of the Monsoon. Copy of the order is filed as Annexure P/5. The said order is in operation till today as the Monsoon has not been arrived so far. The Additional District sh Judge has also taken into consideration the fact that 70% of the work e of laying down the pipe line has been completed and due to the ad summer season the public at large is suffering due to scarcity of the Pr water in the city. The Additional District Judge has also taken into consideration the fact that the mine of the plaintiff is not functioning a and more than hundred times water is available in the mine as hy compare to the need of the plaintiff. The balance of convenience is ad found in favour of the general public for whom the M defendant/municipal corporation is laying down the pipe lines to draw the water from the mine of the plaintiff. of The plaintiff is not having any independent source of water and the water of rain are being collected in the mine therefor, prima facie rt the plaintiff cannot claim ownership over the water. The State ou Government came up with the enactment called the Adhiniyam, 1986 C in order to provide for preservation of the water and water sources and h for regulation of digging of tube-wells in order to maintain the water ig supply to the public for domestic purposes and for matters ancillary H thereto. The aims and objects of this enactment are reproduced herein below:- "Statements of Objects and Reasons- Water is the most essential for life for all living being. During drought season supply of water to the general public is the problem of the State. It is, therefore considered necessary to preserve water in the water sources for the consumption and use of human beings, namely for drinking, bathing, washing, cleaning as also for domestic animals. It is, also considered necessary to regulate the digging of tube-wells in order to ensure the water supply to the public it is, therefore, proposed to enact a suitable legislative measure to preserve the water sources in the water scarcity areas of the State." In this Act section 2 (d) defines the "water sources". The 'water sources' means river, dam, canal, stream, fountain, lake, spring, tank, anicuts or well from which the State Government or local authority supply water to the public under its water supply scheme for domestic sh purposes. Section 4-A authorizes the Collector to requisition e temporarily any water sources situated in the water scarcity area. As ad per the proviso 4-A adequate quantity of water will continue to be Pr supplied to the owner or to the person in possession of the water source for domestic purposes. The owner is entitled for payment of a hy rent and damages for the requisition of water sources. Section 4 puts restriction to take the water for irrigation or industrial or any other ad purposes except the domestic purposes without the permission of the M Collector and according to which no person shall take water by any means from any water source in the water scarcity area for irrigation of or industrial or any other purposes except the domestic purposes rt without the permission of the Collector concerned. ou Therefore, in view of Sections 4 , 4-A of the Adhiniyam, 1986 if the private source of water has been temporarily requisitioned by the C Collector then the owner is required to take permission from the h Collector under Section 4 of the Adhiniyam, 1986 to use the said ig water for domestic purposes only. Vide order dated 1/02/2017 the H Collector has already issued a declaration under Section 3 of the Adhiniyam, 1986. Once the declaration has been made then under Section 4 and 4-A , the water can be used only for domestic purposes and not for industrial or irrigation purposes. Therefore, the plaintiff himself is restrained by virtue of section 4 and proviso of section 4-A to use his own water other than domestic purposes. Under the Adhiniyam, 1986 there is a provision of appeal which provides that if any person is aggrieved by the order passed by the Collector under sections 4 and 4-A , he may prefer an appeal to the Commissioner. In view of the aforesaid, I do not find any reason to interfere with the impugned order in this writ petition. Accordingly, the writ petition is dismissed. (VIVEK RUSIA) JUDGE Digitally signed by NAVEEN NAGDEVE Date: 2018.04.18 17:19:58 +05'30' navin e sh ad Pr a hy ad M of rt ou C h ig H
